description: macOS notch utility app — Dynamic Island-style expandable widget surface over the MacBook notch

userStories.title: NSPanel subclass, screen detector, and notch state model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational window classes: a borderless non-activating NSPanel, NSScreen extensions for notch detection/geometry, and an observable state model.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Window/NotchPanel.swift exists with class NotchPanel: NSPanel configured as borderless, non-activating, no shadow, clear background, statusBar+1 level, canJoinAllSpaces+stationary+fullScreenAuxiliary collection behavior, hidesOnDeactivate=false, ignoresMouseEvents=true

NotchNook/Window/ScreenDetector.swift exists with NSScreen extension providing hasNotch (checks auxiliaryTopLeftArea/auxiliaryTopRightArea), notchSize, notchFrame, virtualPillFrame (180pt wide), overlayFrame, and stableIdentifier properties
NotchNook/Models/NotchState.swift exists with @Observable class containing Phase enum (idle, expanding, expanded, tray, hud) and screenHasNotch bool
swiftc -typecheck passes for all three files with proper imports
userStories.notes: Phase 1 Plan 02 Task 1. Do NOT include .fullSizeContentView in style mask. Do NOT use .moveToActiveSpace. canBecomeKey returns true, canBecomeMain returns false.

userStories.title: Window manager, root view, and AppDelegate wiring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NotchWindowManager to create per-screen overlay panels, a SwiftUI root view hosted inside, and AppDelegate to initialize the manager on launch.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Views/NotchRootView.swift exists with SwiftUI view accepting NotchState, rendering dark rounded rectangle with corner radius 10 and centered 'NotchNook' caption text

NotchNook/Window/NotchWindowManager.swift exists with windows dictionary keyed by stableIdentifier, synchronizeWindows() with 500ms debounce, createWindow(for:) using NSHostingView, repositionWindow(_:on:), and observer for didChangeScreenParametersNotification
NotchNook/AppDelegate.swift modified to instantiate NotchWindowManager in applicationDidFinishLaunching
swiftc -typecheck passes for all modified/created files
userStories.notes: Phase 1 Plan 02 Task 2. Panel content is NSHostingView wrapping NotchRootView. orderFront(nil) to show. Remove windows when screen disconnects (orderOut first).

userStories.title: Mouse tracking and hover detection for notch expansion
userStories.description: As a user, I want hovering over the notch area for 200ms to trigger expansion, so I can interact with widgets.
acceptanceCriteria: NSTrackingArea added to NotchPanel that detects mouseEntered/mouseExited events
200ms dwell timer before triggering expansion (configurable via UserDefaults, default 200ms)
ignoresMouseEvents toggled to false when tracking is active
Mouse exit from expanded area starts 300ms grace timer before collapsing
NotchState.phase transitions: idle -> expanding on hover dwell
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2 Plan 01. Use NSEvent.addLocalMonitorForEvents and NSTrackingArea. Global monitor requires Accessibility permission — request lazily.

userStories.title: Spring animations for expand/collapse transitions
userStories.description: As a user, I want smooth 60fps spring animations when the notch expands and collapses.
acceptanceCriteria: Expanding animates panel frame from collapsed (notch-sized) to expanded (600pt wide default) with spring timing
Collapsing reverses the animation smoothly back to notch dimensions
NotchState.phase transitions through expanding -> expanded and back to idle
Animation runs at 60fps with no visual tearing
Panel frame change uses NSAnimationContext or SwiftUI withAnimation(.spring())
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. Expanded width configurable (400-800pt, default 600pt). Use screen.overlayFrame for collapsed and calculated expanded frame centered on screen.

userStories.title: Custom notch shape with concave cutout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the expanded notch to blend seamlessly with the hardware notch edges using a custom shape.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Views/NotchShape.swift exists with SwiftUI Shape conformance
Shape draws rounded rectangle with concave cutout at top matching hardware notch profile
On non-notch Macs, shape is a simple capsule (fully rounded rectangle)
Shape animatable between collapsed and expanded sizes
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. The concave top edge has two inward curves flanking the camera area. Use Bezier path with control points for smooth curves.

userStories.title: Vibrancy background and dismissal behaviors
userStories.description: As a user, I want frosted glass vibrancy in expanded state and multiple ways to dismiss the notch.
acceptanceCriteria: Expanded state shows NSVisualEffectView bridged into SwiftUI with .hudWindow material and .behindWindow blending
Collapsed state uses opaque black fill matching hardware notch
Animated transition between opaque (collapsed) and vibrancy (expanded)
Clicking outside the expanded notch dismisses immediately
Pressing Escape dismisses immediately
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. Bridge NSVisualEffectView via NSViewRepresentable. Escape key requires panel to be key window momentarily.

userStories.title: Activation trigger settings (hover, click, hotkey)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose how I activate the notch — hover, click, or keyboard shortcut.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > General tab has activation trigger picker: Hover (default), Click, Keyboard Shortcut
Hover delay slider (100ms-500ms) shown when Hover is selected
Global hotkey configuration (default Option+N) shown when Keyboard Shortcut is selected
Changing trigger immediately updates behavior without app restart
Settings persisted via UserDefaults
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. Add to existing SettingsView TabView. Global hotkey uses NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents with key combo matching.

userStories.title: MediaRemote private framework service layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a media service that fetches now-playing data via MediaRemote with fallback to MPNowPlayingInfoCenter.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Services/MediaService.swift exists with protocol-based abstraction
MediaRemote functions loaded dynamically via dlopen/dlsym from MediaRemote.framework
Service provides: track title, artist, album art (as NSImage), playback state, app bundle ID
Play/pause, next, previous commands work via MRMediaRemoteSendCommand
Fallback to MPNowPlayingInfoCenter if MediaRemote unavailable
Registers for MRMediaRemoteRegisterForNowPlayingNotifications to get live updates
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 3. Use @objc dynamic loading for MediaRemote. Abstract behind a MediaServiceProtocol so fallback is transparent.

userStories.title: Media player expanded widget view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see album art, track info, and playback controls when the notch is expanded.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Views/Widgets/MediaPlayerWidget.swift exists
Displays album art (rounded corners), track title, artist name
Shows playback progress indicator
Play/pause, next, previous buttons that call MediaService
Shows source app icon (Spotify, Apple Music, etc.) from bundle ID
Widget only appears when media is actively playing or paused
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 3. Widget should be sized to fit within expanded notch (~600pt wide panel). Use AsyncImage or NSImage for album art.

userStories.title: Collapsed media live activity indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a subtle waveform animation and album art peek when music is playing and the notch is collapsed.
acceptanceCriteria: When media is playing and notch is collapsed, show small waveform animation flanking the notch
Album art peek visible as tiny circle next to notch edge
Animation uses TimelineView only when media is actively playing (paused = static)
Waveform stops when no media is playing to save CPU
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 3. Keep animations minimal for performance. Use drawingGroup() for Metal rendering.

userStories.title: Media gesture controls (swipe to skip)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to swipe horizontally on the notch to skip tracks.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-finger horizontal swipe inside expanded notch area skips to next (right) or previous (left) track
Swipe gesture uses NSEvent scrollWheel monitoring or SwiftUI gesture recognizers
Haptic feedback (if available) on successful skip
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 3. Detect horizontal scroll events with sufficient delta threshold to distinguish from vertical scroll.

userStories.title: Volume monitoring and HUD slider view
userStories.description: As a user, I want volume changes to show a slim slider inside the notch instead of the native macOS HUD.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Services/HUDService.swift monitors volume changes via CoreAudio AudioObjectAddPropertyListener on default output device
NotchNook/Views/HUDOverlayView.swift renders slim horizontal slider showing current volume level
Pressing volume key shows slider inside notch area with smooth animation
Slider auto-dismisses after 2 seconds (configurable 1-5s)
User can grab and drag slider to adjust volume interactively
NotchState.phase transitions to .hud and back to previous state on dismiss
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 4. Use kAudioHardwareServiceDeviceProperty_VirtualMainVolume for volume. HUD takes priority over all other states.

userStories.title: Brightness monitoring and HUD slider
userStories.description: As a user, I want brightness changes to show a slim slider inside the notch instead of the native macOS HUD.
acceptanceCriteria: HUDService extended to monitor brightness changes via IOKit display services (IODisplayGetFloatParameter)
Brightness slider rendered alongside volume slider in HUDOverlayView
Pressing brightness key shows brightness slider inside notch with smooth animation
Same auto-dismiss and interactive drag behavior as volume slider
HUD type indicator (speaker icon vs sun icon) distinguishes volume from brightness
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 4. Use CoreDisplay or IOKit for brightness reading. May need polling if no notification API available.

userStories.title: Native HUD suppression (best-effort)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to suppress the native macOS volume/brightness HUD so it doesn't overlap with our custom one.
acceptanceCriteria: Best-effort suppression of native macOS HUD when custom HUD is active
Uses NSEvent.addGlobalMonitorForEvents(matching: .systemDefined) to detect OSD trigger keys
Graceful fallback: if suppression fails, custom HUD displays alongside native (not a blocker)
HUD replacement is opt-in via Settings toggle (default: on)
Settings > HUD tab with enable/disable toggle and auto-dismiss timeout slider
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 4. Native HUD suppression is fragile across OS versions. Make it degrade gracefully. Requires Accessibility permission.

userStories.title: Frontmost app detection and widget switching
userStories.description: As a user, I want the notch to automatically show relevant widgets based on which app is in front.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Services/ContextService.swift monitors frontmost app via NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication
Observes didActivateApplicationNotification for real-time app switching
Widget visibility rules: e.g., show media widget when Spotify/Music is frontmost
Default rules built-in, user can override in settings
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 5. Depends on Phase 3 (media widget) and Phase 4 (HUD) being available to switch between.

userStories.title: Time-of-day profiles and Focus Mode integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want widgets to change based on time of day and active Focus Mode.
acceptanceCriteria: ContextService supports time-based profiles: morning, afternoon, evening (configurable time boundaries)
Detects active macOS Focus Mode via NSDoNotDisturb or FocusFilter API
Different widget sets shown per Focus Mode (e.g., Work focus hides media, shows calendar)
Settings UI for configuring time profiles and Focus Mode widget assignments
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 5. Use DistributedNotificationCenter for Focus Mode changes if public API insufficient.

userStories.title: Custom context rules engine and settings UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create custom rules like 'When Xcode is frontmost, show shortcuts widget'.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Models/ContextRule.swift with Codable model: condition (app, time, focus) + widget set
Settings > Context tab with rule list, add/edit/delete functionality
Rule conditions: When [app is X / time is Y / Focus Mode is Z] show [widget set]
Rules evaluated in priority order, first match wins
Rules persisted to UserDefaults or JSON file
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 5. Keep rule engine simple — condition+action pairs, no complex boolean logic for v1.

userStories.title: Drop zone UI and drag detection on notch
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag files over the notch and see a drop zone appear.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a file over the notch area reveals a visual drop zone UI with pulsing highlight
Uses registerForDraggedTypes on NotchPanel for file URL types
NotchState.phase transitions to .tray when drag enters
Drop zone shows icon and text hint ('Drop to process')
Releasing drag outside cancels gracefully
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 6. Register for NSPasteboard.PasteboardType.fileURL. Use NSDraggingDestination protocol on panel.

userStories.title: Built-in drop actions (compress image, OCR)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drop an image on the notch to compress/resize it or extract text via OCR.
acceptanceCriteria: NotchNook/Services/DropActionService.swift with DropAction protocol
Built-in CompressImageAction: accepts image files, compresses/resizes, copies result to clipboard
Built-in OCRAction: accepts image files, extracts text via Vision framework VNRecognizeTextRequest, copies to clipboard
Action selection UI shown after drop (if multiple actions match the file type)
Success/failure feedback shown briefly in notch
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 6. Use NSImage for resize, Vision framework for OCR. Copy results to NSPasteboard.general.

userStories.title: Shortcuts integration and shell pipeline support
userStories.description: As a user, I want to assign macOS Shortcuts or shell scripts as drop actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Drop action can invoke a macOS Shortcut by name via shortcuts:// URL scheme
Drop action can run a shell script/command with the dropped file path as argument
Multi-step pipeline support: chain actions sequentially (action1 output -> action2 input)
Pipeline configuration stored as JSON/Codable model
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 6. Use Process() for shell scripts. Pass file path as $1 argument. Capture stdout for pipeline chaining.

userStories.title: Drop action configuration UI in settings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure drop actions and pipelines in settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > Drop Actions tab listing all configured actions
Add/edit/delete drop actions with type picker (built-in, Shortcut, shell script)
Pipeline builder: drag to reorder steps, add/remove steps
File type filter per action (images only, PDFs only, any file)
Drop actions persisted to UserDefaults or JSON file
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 6. Keep UI simple — list of actions with inline editing.

userStories.title: CPU and memory optimization pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to profile and optimize the app to hit the performance budget.
acceptanceCriteria: Idle CPU usage below 0.5% when collapsed with no media playing (verify via Activity Monitor or Instruments)
Active CPU usage below 3% when expanded with widgets visible
Memory usage below 50MB during normal operation
No unnecessary timers or polling when idle — lazy widget loading
TimelineView only active during media playback animation
drawingGroup() applied to complex animated views
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 7. Profile with Instruments (Time Profiler, Allocations). Eliminate any retain cycles or leaked observers.

userStories.title: Sleep/wake resilience and display hotplug hardening
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to survive sleep/wake cycles and monitor connect/disconnect without bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: App survives sleep/wake cycles: overlay repositions correctly after wake
Listen for NSWorkspace.willSleepNotification and didWakeNotification
On wake: re-run synchronizeWindows() with delay to let screen parameters settle
Display hotplug: connecting external monitor creates overlay, disconnecting removes it cleanly
No crashes on rapid connect/disconnect cycles
Window positions correct after display arrangement changes in System Settings
swiftc -typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 7. Add 1-2 second delay after wake before re-syncing windows. Test with Sidecar if external monitor unavailable.
